creator: destination_wellknown
caption:
Important info if you visit Copenhagen in 2024👇

I live in Copenhagen, and I think it’s one of the best travel destinations in the summertime, but there are a few things tourists need to know before coming here:

6) All the travel articles and guides that mention Freetown Christiania are outdated. 🍃has never been legal anywhere in Denmark, but you used to be able to buy & smoke ‘freely’ in Christiania. Those days are over; the police are now patrolling the area and handing out massive fines. 

5) Tivoli Hotel is nowhere near the Tivoli Gardens - the amusement park is right in the heart of the city centre and well worth a visit, but the hotel is a good few kilometres away.

4) The Little Mermaid is a tiny statue with nothing to do around it. It’s one of the worst tourist attractions in Copenhagen. 

3) CPH is not the place to learn how to ride a bike. It might look super fun, but if you’ve never ridden a bike before, please don’t rent a bike as a tourist in Copenhagen. It can be pretty dangerous and rush hour in the bike lanes is extremely hectic - and I say this as a Dane who lives here and bikes everywhere.

2) You don’t have to take out cash. Nobody uses cash anymore; you can just pay by card or mobile. If you have some cash, a law states that the shop must take it even though they’ll probably look at you funny.

1) Please don’t eat at the restaurants at Strøget (Europe’s longest pedestrianised shopping street) - these places are only made for tourists who don’t know where to go. But it’s 2024, so just load up Google Maps, type in “restaurant”, and find a good place in one of the side streets) 🤠

Follow for more Copenhagen travel tips, and let me know if you have any questions 🇩🇰 I’ve also got quite a few travel guides over on my website and Youtube; there’s a link to both in my bio ✌️  #copenhagen #denmarktravel

transcription.text: Now he's thinking about me every night, oh, and he says to me, like, it's so sick.
